scores.notes: Many honorifics ('Dr.', 'Professor', 'President'), context phrases ('coal use', 'London-based think tank', 'solar power capacity'), non-standard NUM type; 'Denmark' has no literal match in source text; Saudi Aramco as PROD instead of ORG.
Five clean atomic English tags; misses lobbying, COP30, deforestation, and India-specific topics.
Attributes the CO2 record (37.4 Gt) to IPCC rather than Global Carbon Project; misses lobbying, renewable energy highlights, EU progress, and COP30.

Honorifics retained; non-entity phrases ('Climate activists', 'monument') extracted; non-standard NUM type throughout; 'Deense windenergiebedrijf' and 'Spaanse Iberdrola' include context prefixes; 'VN-secretaris-generaal' mistyped as ORG.
Six clean atomic English tags; misses lobbying, COP30, and deforestation.
No factual errors in three bullets; very incomplete — misses lobbying, COP30, EU progress, India, and renewable energy highlights.

Verbose forms ('Danish wind energy company Ørsted', 'COP30 summit in Belém, Brazil'); 'former European Commission President' extracted as PER (role phrase not a name); non-standard EVENT type used; Paris Agreement and European Green Deal as PROD (borderline).
'nationally-determined-contributions' is verbose but precise; misses deforestation and India-specific topics.
No errors; first bullet omits the specific 1.45°C figure; misses CO2 record, EU 32%, Amazon deforestation, and renewable energy highlights.

'196 landen' is a quantifier phrase not a named entity; 'Europees Commissievoorzitter Ursula von der Leyen' retains title; PROD overused for agreements and reports (Akkoord van Parijs, Zesde Beoordelingsrapport, COP30-top); INPE duplicated.
'copolitical-will' is a malformed typo tag; otherwise good breadth.
'India's emissions are second only to China and the US' implies second place but India is third; misses CO2 record, EU 32%, deforestation stats, and renewable energy progress.

Compound tags: 'fossil-fuel-lobbying', 'international-environmental-policy', 'deforestation-amazon'; good topic breadth compensates.

No errors; covers signing context, temperature, CO2, deforestation, lobbying, India criticism, and COP30; misses EU 32% reduction and renewable energy highlights.

'Akkord van Parijs' is a misspelling of 'Akkoord van Parijs' from the source; 'Australische belangengroep' is a context phrase; Saudi Aramco, Shell, BP, ExxonMobil, Ørsted, Iberdrola tagged as PROD; 'VN-secretaris-generaal' as MISC is a title not an entity.

No errors; comprehensive coverage including temperature, CO2, EU 32%, deforestation, lobbying, renewable energy (solar, battery costs, 430 GW); only minor gap is COP30 details and Guterres NDC call.

Honorifics retained ('Dr.', 'Professor'); 'Australian advocacy group' and 'London-based think tank' are context phrases not named entities; Global Carbon Project, InfluenceMap, and BloombergNEF misclassified as PROD.
Clean atomic tags; 'sustainability' is overly generic; otherwise good breadth.
No errors; includes key figures (196 nations, 1.45°C, 37.4 Gt, 32%); misses lobbying amount, renewable energy highlights, COP30, and India's 2070 target specifically.
PROD type severely overused for abstracts: IEA, Akkoord van Parijs, COP30-top, NDC, INPE all tagged PROD; 'dr. Katharine Hayhoe' retains honorific; no invented entities.
'eu-green-deal' and 'carbon-neutrality' are compound; 'solar-power' and 'wind-energy' add useful specificity; misses lobbying.
No errors but very sparse; only $750M is a specific figure; no temperature readings, CO2 record, deforestation stats, COP30, or renewable energy highlights.

'March 2025' is invented (source mentions September 2025 and November 2025 only); China and United States tagged as ORG; Imperial College London as LOC; companies as PROD; 'President' and 'Australian' extracted as standalone non-entities; 'Danish wind energy company Ørsted' and 'Spain's Iberdrola' retain context prefixes.
Only six tags; missing global-warming, lobbying, COP30, deforestation, and emissions; the six present are relevant and atomic.
No errors; includes 196 nations, Le Bourget, Dec 12 2015, 37.4 Gt, 32%, $750M; misses temperature reading (1.45°C), India's 2070 target, renewable energy highlights, and COP30.

Grantham Institute extracted twice with conflicting types (ORG and PROD); 'Londense denktank' and 'Europees Commissievoorzitter' are context phrases; Youth Climate Coalition as MISC; companies as PROD; Ørsted, Iberdrola, BloombergNEF omitted.
'copenhagen-summit' is hallucinated — the Copenhagen summit is not mentioned anywhere in the source; only five tags total, missing most topics.
No errors; five bullets including 1.45°C, 11568 km², 32%, 2070; misses lobbying ($750M), COP30, and renewable energy specifics.

Honorifics retained ('Dr.', 'Professor'); 'Australian advocacy group Climate Council' merges descriptor with entity name; generally correct types; missing India, The Nature Conservancy, Texas Tech, Katharine Hayhoe, European Union.

Two long prose bullets rather than specific facts; 'fossil fuel companies spend millions' understates the $750M figure; 'In 2025' framing is slightly misleading for the 2024 temperature data; covers most storylines but lacks specific numbers.

'University of Exeter' extracted in English from Dutch text (source has 'Universiteit van Exeter'); 'IPCC' as MISC instead of ORG; 'Paris' extracted in English where source says 'Parijs'; 'European Green Deal' in English from Dutch text.

'nationally-determined-contributions' is verbose; solid breadth; misses deforestation and India-specific topics.

Eight detailed bullets covering all major storylines with specific figures (1.45°C, 37.4 Gt, 32%, $750M, 2 terawatts, November 2025); only minor vagueness is 'battery storage costs falling significantly' instead of 89%.

'ipcc-report' compounds org and document type; otherwise good atomic English tags; misses deforestation and India-specific topics.
